currentUse: Active Roman Catholic parish church of the Archdiocese of Chicago, operating publicly as St. Anthony / San Antonio de Padua Parish, 1510 S. 49th Court, Cicero, IL 60804 — a predominantly Spanish-language congregation, with a Sunday-morning Lithuanian Mass still listed in Mass-time directories
summary: St. Anthony's (Šv. Antano) in Cicero is one of the survival stories: the 1925 Romanesque Revival church still stands at 1510 S. 49th Court and remains an active, structurally independent parish of the Archdiocese of Chicago — the property was never sold or closed. Today it operates as St. Anthony / San Antonio de Padua, serving an overwhelmingly Spanish-speaking congregation with roughly six Spanish Masses each Sunday alongside one English and one Lithuanian Mass; Mass-time directories list the Lithuanian Mass at 8:45 or 9:00 a.m. Sunday. The parish went through the Archdiocese's Renew My Church restructuring in its North Berwyn-Cicero grouping (with Mary Queen of Heaven, St. Odilo, and Our Lady of the Mount) in November 2021 and emerged untouched: St. Anthony was left structurally independent while the two neighboring Cicero parishes were merged effective July 1, 2022. The Archdiocese of Chicago parish directory (August 2025 and March 2026 editions) lists Rev. Ismael Sandoval as pastor. The building's Lithuanian fabric survives in place: a side altar to Blessed Jurgis Matulaitis, a Vytis stained-glass window, a traditional Lithuanian chapel-post, Lithuanian inscriptions under the Stations of the Cross, and the adjacent former Lithuanian school building with its Lithuanian name carved above the entrance; some Lithuanians reportedly still live in the area and attend. The parish maintains a live website (sanantoniocicero.net) publishing weekly Spanish-language bulletins.

developments.headline: St. Anthony emerges from Renew My Church structurally independent
developments.detail: In the Archdiocese of Chicago's Renew My Church process, the North Berwyn-Cicero grouping comprised St. Anthony, Mary Queen of Heaven, St. Odilo, and Our Lady of the Mount. The November 2021 decision merged Mary Queen of Heaven and Our Lady of the Mount into one parish with two worship sites (effective July 1, 2022; renamed Our Lady, the Mystical Rose Parish on January 26, 2023), while St. Anthony and St. Odilo were left structurally independent and encouraged to collaborate with the merged parish.

sources.title: History of Mary Queen of Heaven Church / Our Lady, the Mystical Rose Parish
sources.publisher: Our Lady, the Mystical Rose Parish (olmrparish.org)

developments.headline: Rev. Ismael Sandoval listed as pastor in archdiocesan directory
developments.detail: The Archdiocese of Chicago Parish Directory (August 2025 edition, carried forward in the March 2026 edition) lists Rev. Ismael Sandoval as pastor of St. Anthony, 1510 S. 49th Ct., Cicero, with Rev. Alejandro Garrido as resident. Note: the directory PDF itself is behind a bot-block (HTTP 403); the listing was confirmed only via search-result excerpts of the directory.

sources.title: The Archdiocese of Chicago Parish Directory, March 2026

developments.headline: Sunday Lithuanian Mass still listed among a predominantly Spanish schedule
developments.detail: Mass-time directories currently list one Lithuanian Mass each Sunday morning at St. Anthony — 8:45 a.m. per CatholicMassTime.org and DiscoverMass, 9:00 a.m. per Catholic Church Directory — alongside one English Mass and six Spanish Masses (6:30 a.m., 10:15 a.m., 11:45 a.m., 1:15 p.m., 2:45 p.m., 7:00 p.m.) plus bilingual weekday Masses. Listings are undated aggregator data; on-the-ground currency of the Lithuanian Mass was not independently confirmed.

sources.title: St. Anthony's Church Mass Times - Cicero, Illinois
St. Anthony Parish, 1510 S. 49th Court, Cicero, IL

developments.headline: Lithuanian heritage fabric documented intact inside the standing 1925 church
developments.detail: The Global True Lithuania heritage survey describes the massive Romanesque Revival church (cornerstone 1925) as still in active use, with a side altar to Blessed Jurgis Matulaitis (who blessed the church), a stained-glass window bearing the Vytis, a traditional Lithuanian chapel-post, Lithuanian inscriptions under the old Stations of the Cross and over the Virgin Mary statue, and the adjacent former Lithuanian school building with its Lithuanian name chiseled above the entrance. It notes some Lithuanians still live in Cicero and attend the church, though the district is now predominantly Hispanic.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. Three refinements: (1) The archive's "Lithuanian Masses continued through at least Dec 2019" can likely be extended — multiple current Mass-time directories (undated aggregator listings) and the Global True Lithuania heritage survey still list/describe a weekly Sunday Lithuanian Mass, though no dated 2024-2026 primary source was found confirming it is actually still celebrated. (2) The archive's "survived inside the diocese" is confirmed and strengthened: the parish passed through the Archdiocese's Renew My Church restructuring (North Berwyn-Cicero grouping, decision November 2021) and was left structurally independent while two neighboring Cicero parishes were merged — a restructuring exposure event the archive record does not mention. (3) Consistent with the archive's note of the 2011 transfer to the Mexican community, the parish now publicly brands itself "San Antonio de Padua," its website and bulletins are Spanish-language, and the schedule is dominated by Spanish Masses.
gaps: The pastor listing (Rev. Ismael Sandoval, with Rev. Alejandro Garrido in residence) comes from the Archdiocese of Chicago Parish Directory (Aug 2025 / Mar 2026), but archchicago.org blocks automated fetching (HTTP 403 / Cloudflare), so it was confirmed only through search-engine excerpts of the directory, not the opened PDF — treat as probable, not verified. The currency of the Lithuanian Mass is the biggest gap: directory aggregators disagree on the time (8:45 vs. 9:00 a.m.) and carry no last-verified dates; the parish's own Wix site is JavaScript-rendered and its bulletin pages could not be text-extracted, so no dated 2024-2026 primary confirmation of an actual Lithuanian liturgy was obtained. No recent Draugas coverage of the Cicero community (2022-2026) surfaced in web search. The 1968 Lithuanian Independence marker at the church (hmdb.org m=244737) appeared in search results but the page itself was Cloudflare-blocked, so it is excluded from the findings. The Archdiocese's own November 2021 RMC news release (archchicago.org) and Chicago Catholic coverage were also 403-blocked; the RMC outcome is sourced instead from the merged neighboring parish's history page. No property sale, listing, demolition, or preservation-threat coverage was found — consistent with the building remaining a functioning archdiocesan parish.
